evals.qualitative_notes: Both produce near-identical commit messages. with_skill adds structured Analysis section with decision rationale; without_skill is more concise.

with_skill uses functional domain scope (provider-health) and Chinese body matching Kairos conventions. without_skill uses layer scope (console) and English body. Both valid, with_skill shows better convention awareness.

analysis.cross_project_finding: commit-msg-generator shows EQUAL verdict on Kairos — vanilla Claude already produces good Conventional Commits for clear, well-described changes. The Skill's internal Aria delta (+0.80) was measured on more nuanced scenarios (scope detection, mode selection). For straightforward external projects, the Skill adds convention awareness but not pass-rate improvement.
qualitative_advantages: with_skill produces Analysis section explaining decisions (useful for team review)
with_skill chose more precise functional domain scopes (provider-health vs console)
with_skill matched Kairos's Chinese description convention in eval 2
with_skill uses ~22% more tokens but provides richer context
analysis.recommendation: commit-msg-generator has qualitative value (decision rationale, convention matching) but not quantitative delta on simple Kairos scenarios. Test with more complex/ambiguous scenarios for stronger differentiation.

comparison_to_internal.explanation: Aria internal evals test edge cases (scope ambiguity, multi-module commits). Kairos evals use clear descriptions where vanilla Claude performs equally well.
